name: Pengrowth Energy Corporation
description: Pengrowth Energy Corporation was a prominent Canadian oil and natural gas company headquartered in Calgary, Alberta. Established in 1988 by entrepreneur James S. Kinnear, the firm originally operated as one of the largest Canadian royalty trusts. It focused on the development of conventional oil and natural gas assets, with a significant emphasis on bitumen production from the Lindbergh thermal project. Following years of operational challenges and financial restructuring, the company was acquired by Cona Resources Ltd. in January 2020, effectively ending its status as an independent, publicly traded entity on the Toronto Stock Exchange.
